From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [World Bowls Events](/w/index.php?title=World_Bowls_Events&redirect=no "World Bowls Events"))

World lawn bowls list and schedule of events

These are the premier **International Bowls Events** between national [bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") organisations affiliated to [World Bowls](/wiki/World_Bowls "World Bowls"), the [PBA](/wiki/Professional_Bowls_Association "Professional Bowls Association"), [World Bowls Tour](/wiki/World_Bowls_Tour "World Bowls Tour") and the [IIBC](/wiki/International_Indoor_Bowls_Council "International Indoor Bowls Council").

## Calendar

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Calendar")]

### Outdoor

| [World Singles Champion of Champions](/wiki/World_Singles_Champion_of_Champions "World Singles Champion of Champions") | [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") Barham, NSW | 6–10 October 2026 |

## World Outdoor Bowls Championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: World Outdoor Bowls Championships")]

Main article: [World Bowls Championship](/wiki/World_Bowls_Championship "World Bowls Championship")

The sport's premier event and organised by World Bowls. First held in 1966, the World Outdoor Bowls Championships for men and women are held every 4 years. From 2008 the men's and women's events are held together. Qualifying national bowls organisations (usually countries) are represented by a team of 5 players, who play once as a single and a four, then again as a pair and a triple. Gold, silver, and bronze medals are awarded in each of the 4 disciplines, and there is also a trophy for the best overall team — the Leonard Trophy for men and the Taylor Trophy for women.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-SULL-4)

## Commonwealth Games

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Commonwealth Games")]

Main article: [Lawn bowls at the Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_Commonwealth_Games "Lawn bowls at the Commonwealth Games")

Organised by the [Commonwealth Games Federation](/wiki/Commonwealth_Games_Federation "Commonwealth Games Federation"). First held in 1930, the Commonwealth Games Bowls Championships for men and women are held every 4 years. Women's events were added from 1982. Four gold medals are competed for in the singles, pairs, triples and fours.

## World Indoor Bowls Championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: World Indoor Bowls Championships")]

Main article: [World Indoor Bowls Championships](/wiki/World_Indoor_Bowls_Championships "World Indoor Bowls Championships")

One of two World Championship indoor events. Organised by the PBA/World Bowls Tour. First held in 1979, the World Indoor Bowls Championships for men and women are held every year. There are currently five events, the open singles, the open pairs, the women's singles, the mixed pairs and open under-25 singles.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[4]](#cite_note-SULL-4) It is currently organised by the [Professional Bowls Association](/wiki/Professional_Bowls_Association "Professional Bowls Association") and the [World Bowls Tour](/wiki/World_Bowls_Tour "World Bowls Tour") and involves mainly Northern Hemisphere nations.

## World Bowls Indoor Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: World Bowls Indoor Championship")]

Main article: [World Bowls Indoor Championships](/wiki/World_Bowls_Indoor_Championships "World Bowls Indoor Championships")

One of two World Championship indoor events. Organised jointly by World Bowls and the IIBC. A new event starting in 2022 (a merger of the former World Cup Singles and IIBC championships. Rival competition to the PBA and WBT event listed above.

## World Singles Champion of Champions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: World Singles Champion of Champions")]

Main article: [World Singles Champion of Champions](/wiki/World_Singles_Champion_of_Champions "World Singles Champion of Champions")

Organised by World Bowls. A leading outdoor event held annually between the winners of the respective National Championships.

## Pacific Games

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Pacific Games")]

Main article: [Lawn bowls at the Pacific Games](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_Pacific_Games "Lawn bowls at the Pacific Games")

Organised by Pacific Games Council. A leading outdoor event held every four years between the Pacific Islands nations as part of the [Pacific Games](/wiki/Pacific_Games "Pacific Games").

## Southeast Asian Games

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Southeast Asian Games")]

Main article: [Lawn bowls at the Southeast Asian Games](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_Southeast_Asian_Games "Lawn bowls at the Southeast Asian Games")

Organised by Southeast Asian Games Federation. A leading outdoor event held every two years between the Southeast Asian nations as part of the [Southeast Asian Games](/wiki/Southeast_Asian_Games "Southeast Asian Games").

## Asian Lawn Bowls Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Asian Lawn Bowls Championship")]

Main article: [Asian Lawn Bowls Championship](/wiki/Asian_Lawn_Bowls_Championship "Asian Lawn Bowls Championship")

Organised by World Bowls. A leading outdoor event held annually between the Asian nations.

## European Bowls Championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: European Bowls Championships")]

Main article: [European Bowls Championships](/wiki/European_Bowls_Championships "European Bowls Championships")

Organised by Bowls Europe Ltd. A leading outdoor event held annually between European nations.

## National Championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: National Championships")]

Main article: [Lawn bowls National Championships](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_National_Championships "Lawn bowls National Championships")

## Other leading events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_lawn_and_indoor_bowls_events&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Other leading events")]

* [Hong Kong International Bowls Classic](/wiki/Hong_Kong_International_Bowls_Classic "Hong Kong International Bowls Classic") (outdoors)
* [Asia Pacific Bowls Championships](/wiki/Asia_Pacific_Bowls_Championships "Asia Pacific Bowls Championships") (outdoors/defunct)
* [Atlantic Bowls Championships](/wiki/Atlantic_Bowls_Championships "Atlantic Bowls Championships") (outdoors/defunct)
* [World Cup Singles](/wiki/World_Cup_Singles "World Cup Singles") (indoors/defunct)
* [Scottish International Open](/wiki/Scottish_International_Open "Scottish International Open") (indoors)
* [World Bowls Junior Indoor Championship](/wiki/World_Bowls_Junior_Indoor_Championship "World Bowls Junior Indoor Championship") (indoors)
